title: MVS 3.8j From a Business Programmer Perspective

sections.title: Logging In to MVS
steps: Open terminal emulator and connect to host:3270
First connection may show logon screen WITHOUT 'Logon ===>' prompt — press Enter to get the prompt
Subsequent connections show logon screen WITH 'Logon ===>' prompt
Enter username at the logon prompt and press Enter
System displays password prompt — enter password and press Enter
System logs you in and displays broadcast screen — press Enter
Fortune screen appears — press Enter to get past it
TSO Applications Menu is displayed

tso_applications_menu.1: RFE — 'SPF like' productivity tool (most used, similar to ISPF)
tso_applications_menu.2: RPF — 'SPF like' productivity tool (older, can be confusing)
tso_applications_menu.6: UTILS — information on utilities and commands available

sections.title: Logging Off MVS
steps: If in RFE menu system, press F3 repeatedly to back out to main menu
Enter X or press F3 to exit menu system and return to TSO command processor
At TSO READY prompt, type LOGOFF and press Enter
Logon screen will be displayed — safe to close terminal
warnings: NEVER close terminal emulator while still logged on — it makes the userid unusable
Recovery option 1: Log on as admin and shutdown the system
Recovery option 2: At Hercules console enter /c u=<userid> to cancel the stuck user

sections.title: Starting MVS 3.8j (TK4-)
steps: Change into the TK4- installation directory
Two run modes: daemon mode (no console) or console mode (recommended)
Run set_console_mode batch file to enable console, or set_daemon_mode for unattended
Windows: type mvs.bat | Linux/Mac: ./mvs
Boot takes a couple of minutes — watch for scrolling output
When the large 'TK4-' banner appears, MVS 3.8J is ready for connections

sections.title: Shutting Down MVS

methods.from_terminal: Log in as admin, get to TSO menu, enter SHUTDOWN, then logoff
methods.from_console: Logoff of the system, then at console enter '/s shutdown' and press Enter

notes: Always shutdown gracefully — improper shutdown can cause boot problems
Shutdown can take up to 5 minutes to complete

sections.title: Using TSO RFE Application
sections.overview: RFE is the primary productivity tool, similar to IBM ISPF. Most administration and programming tasks use RFE.

main_menu.1: Browse — view dataset contents (rarely used directly)
main_menu.2: Edit — look at and edit members of datasets
main_menu.4: Command — execute TSO or CLIST commands without exiting RFE
main_menu.5: Exit — return to TSO Application menu

utilities_menu.1: Library — perform actions on PDS members (display, rename, delete)
utilities_menu.2: DataSet — info, allocate, delete, rename, catalog, uncatalog datasets
utilities_menu.3: Move/Copy — move or copy datasets/members between datasets
utilities_menu.4: DSList — search for datasets by name prefix

edit_commands.s_or_e: Select/edit a member from the member list
edit_commands.d: Delete a member (use 'R' to restore before leaving screen)
edit_commands.save: Save the member (enter in command line)
edit_commands.sub: Submit the member as a JCL job (enter in command line)

fields: Logical Record Length (80 for source PDS)
Physical Block Size (must be multiple of LRECL for blocked)
Number of Directory Blocks (>0 makes it a PDS)

commands.ST *: Show all spooled files (not just current user)
commands.o: Release for output (put queue letter in Q column, usually 'A')

outlist.navigation: Use F8/F7 to page through output

sections.title: Adding a User to the System
sections.overview: TK4- uses RAKF (simplified RACF). Adding a user requires editing the security file AND running the ADDUSER JCL job.
steps: Log on as HERC01
Start RFE → Utilities → DSLIST
Open SYS1.SECURE.CNTL dataset
Edit the 'users' member
Add new line in alphabetical order with format: cols 1-8 USERID, 10-17 Group, 19-26 Password, 28 'N' for ops authority
Save the file
At Hercules console: /stop rakf
Then: /start rakf
Back in RFE: Utilities → DSLIST → SYS2.JCLLIB
Edit ADDUSER member
Change HLQ= to the new username, ensure UTYPE=USER
Save and submit (enter 'sub' in command line)
Log off HERC01, log in as new user

user_groups.USER: Standard user — can work with own files only

sections.rakf_file_format: Fixed format, lines in ascending order. Col 1-8: USERID, Col 10-17: Group, Col 18: * for multi-group, Col 19-26: Password, Col 28: Ops authority (Y/N)
warnings: Do NOT use HERC01 or HERC02 as your regular userid — those are admin accounts
HERC03 and HERC04 are standard USER group accounts

sections.title: Creating Datasets
steps: Log in, select RFE → Utilities (option 3)
Select DATASET (option 2)
Enter 'a' in command field to allocate
Enter dataset name via Project/Library/Type fields OR in DATA SET NAME field
If no quotes around name, userid is prepended automatically
Press Enter to get allocation parameters screen
Fill in: Record Format, Record Length, Block Size, Volume, Unit, Space, Directory Blocks
Press Enter to create
sections.pds_vs_sequential: If Directory Blocks > 0, creates a PDS (like a folder). If 0 or empty, creates a sequential dataset (like a file).

sections.title: JCL Overview

card_format.name: 1-8 characters after //
card_format.parameters: Positional and keyword parameters after operation

JOB.description: Defines the job — first card in job stream

keyword_params.NOTIFY: TSO user to notify on completion
keyword_params.CLASS: Single char scheduling class (A is common)

EXEC.description: Defines a step — PGM=program to execute. Multiple EXEC statements = multiple steps.

DD.description: Data Definition — describes datasets, printers, devices for the EXEC program

sections.continuation: End parameter line with comma, next line starts with // followed by space
sections.end_of_job: // (two slashes alone) marks end of job stream
sections.instream_data: DD * means instream data follows, terminated by /* line

sections.title: Your First COBOL Program
sections.overview: COBOL 65 on MVS 3.8J. Business-oriented language, one of the oldest (after FORTRAN, LISP, ALGOL).

divisions.IDENTIFICATION DIVISION: Required. Contains PROGRAM-ID (required) and optional paragraphs: AUTHOR, INSTALLATION, DATE-WRITTEN, DATE-COMPILED, SECURITY, REMARKS
divisions.ENVIRONMENT DIVISION: Describes hardware communication. CONFIGURATION SECTION (SOURCE-COMPUTER, OBJECT-COMPUTER, SPECIAL-NAMES) and INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION (FILE-CONTROL, I-O-CONTROL)
divisions.DATA DIVISION: FILE SECTION (FD entries for files), WORKING-STORAGE SECTION (variables), LINKAGE SECTION (for subprograms)
divisions.PROCEDURE DIVISION: Program logic. Paragraphs start in cols 8-11, sentences in cols 12-72

device_classes.UR: Card reader, card punch, or printer
device_classes.UT: Tape drive, disk drive (printer also works)

compile_link_go.embedded_source: //COB.SYSIN DD * followed by source, terminated by /*

sections.termination: GOBACK returns to caller, STOP RUN terminates entire program

sections.title: Your First 370 Assembler Program
sections.overview: Closest to hardware, fastest execution. Unique requirement: must establish linkage with calling/called programs.
hello_world: STM   14,12,12(13)    STORE REGISTERS IN CALLERS AREA
ST    13,SAVEAREA+4   STORE ADDRESS OF CALLERS AREA
sections.linkage_boilerplate: Lines 1-13 (save registers, establish base), 14-20 (restore registers, return), and END statement can be reused as a copy member

sections.title: Your First Fortran Program
sections.overview: First compiler (1957, IBM). FORTRAN IV on MVS 3.8J with Fortran G and Fortran H compilers. Heavily used in science/engineering/math.
sections.line_format: Cols 1-6: line ID, Col 7: continuation if non-blank, Cols 8-72: code

compile_link_go.note: FT06F001 — the '6' corresponds to device 6 (WRITE(6,...)) in the program

sections.title: Your First PL/I Program
sections.overview: PL/I (mid-1960s IBM). Designed for math, science, business, and systems programming. Still in use today.
hello_world: SUM = A + B + C + D + E;
sections.notes: Every statement ends with semicolon. !! is string concatenation.

sections.title: Getting TK4- MVS Software
sections.overview: IBM did not copyright MVS in the 1960s-70s. Volker Bandke made MVS 3.8j available for Hercules emulator (by Jay Maynard). Jürgen Winkelmann created TK4- with improvements.
sections.install: Unzip to a directory like 'mainframe' or 'tk4-mvs'
